scenarios.title: Clean puck retrieval
lines: See yourself as the first player back, calm under pressure.
The puck is dumped in behind you.
You shoulder-check over your inside shoulder.
You turn and sprint back for it.
You shoulder-check again — you know where the forecheck is.
You feel him coming, but you are not rushed.
At the puck, you sell up the wall.
The forechecker leans. You cut back into space.
Clean pickup, head up.
Tape-to-tape pass to your winger.

scenarios.title: Walk the blue line
lines: See the puck come to you at the blue line, with the whole play in front of you.
The puck rims around the wall to you at the point.
It's bouncing — you settle it down.
You pull it off the wall, along the blue line.
You open up, eyes on the lane.
A teammate ties up the net-front.
You walk the line one more step, into the seam.
You fire it through — a hard shot, on net, low.
Stick on the ice for the rebound.

scenarios.title: Gap up and stand him up
lines: See the rush coming at you, and feel yourself ready to control the space.
They break out, you swing back.
You skate it backward, eyes on the puck carrier's chest.
You close your gap — one stick-length, then tighter.
He tries to beat you wide.
You match his speed, angle him to the wall.
You stand him up at the line. No entry.
The puck dies in the corner.
Your partner is there. You win it back.

scenarios.title: Calm breakout, D-to-D
lines: See yourself below the goal line, pressure coming, but your head is clear.
You retrieve it low, under pressure from F1.
You shoulder-check — F2 is reading your first option.
You take it behind the net, holding the puck.
D-to-D, hard pass to your partner.
He's got a step now, the forecheck overcommitted.
You skate to support, an option up the wall.
He hits you in stride at the hash marks.
You chip it out, clean. Zone exit, on the tape.

scenarios.title: Long D-zone shift — dig deep, get it out, change
lines: See yourself late in a hard shift, tired but still responsible.
They keep it in at your line — you can't get the clear.
The shift gets long. They cycle low and you're pinned in your zone.
Your legs are burning. You want the change. It's not there yet.
You stay above your check. Box out. Battle for body position.
You don't cheat for offense — you defend, take away the next pass.
The puck squirts loose to your corner.
You get to it, shoulder-check, and chip it hard off the glass and out.
Clean clear. You're first to the bench.
Fresh legs jump on. Long shift, no goal against.
You did your job. That's the change you earned.

scenarios.title: Step up, pinch, and finish
lines: See the puck near the wall and know this is your chance to keep the play alive.
The puck's chipped up the wall, trying to leave the zone.
You're the D at the blue line. You read it early.
You shoulder-check — your center is high, covering for you.
You step down and pinch, pin it on the wall.
You win the battle and knock it to the middle.
You walk off the wall into the slot.
You snap a low shot through the traffic.
It tips in. You kept it alive and buried it.

scenarios.title: Kill a penalty shift
lines: See yourself on the penalty kill, locked into your lane and your job.
You are on the penalty kill. They set up high.
You stay in the shot lane, stick out front.
The puck moves to the half wall. You do not chase.
You keep the box tight and protect the middle.
The shot comes from the point. You get in the lane.
It hits your shin pad and drops loose.
You win the race and chip it hard down the ice.
Your bench taps their sticks. Kill the next one.

scenarios.title: Communicate and quarterback the breakout
lines: See yourself as the calm voice on the ice, helping everyone play faster.
Your partner goes back for the puck.
You shoulder-check and read the forecheck.
You call it early: “Reverse.”
He hears you and banks it behind the net.
You collect it in stride, head up.
The winger is posted on the wall. Your center is low.
You call “wall” and put it on the tape.
Clean exit. Your voice made the game slower.

scenarios.title: Defend the rush, force a bad shot, win the rebound
lines: See the rush building, and feel yourself patient, square, and in control.
They come through the neutral zone with speed.
You hold the middle and match his pace.
You do not lunge. You do not open the inside lane.
You angle him wide toward the boards.
He shoots from outside the dot. Low danger.
Your goalie makes the save. The rebound drops in front.
You tie up his stick and take his body.
Your partner collects the puck.
Middle protected. Rebound won. Job done.

scenarios.title: Win the wall and bury it
lines: See the puck go into your corner and know you are going to win the race.
The puck's chipped into your corner.
You get there first, low and strong.
You take the body, pin your check, protect the puck.
You pull it off the wall to the middle.
You drive the net, hard, inside hand.
You go to the backhand, around the goalie's pad.
The puck crosses the line.
The puck is in. You earned that one.

scenarios.title: Give-and-go through the seam
lines: See open ice ahead of you and feel your speed starting to build.
You carry it through the neutral zone, speed building.
You shoulder-check — your winger is filling the lane.
You give it to him and accelerate.
You hit the soft spot in the D, between the two defenders.
His return pass is on your tape, flat.
One touch to settle it, the goalie sliding.
You roof it over the blocker.
Top corner. Bar down.

scenarios.title: Backcheck and strip
lines: See the turnover happen and feel yourself commit to getting back hard.
The puck turns over at their blue line.
They're on the rush, you're the high forward.
You turn and skate, full speed, back through center.
You track the puck-carrier on his inside hand.
You catch him at the top of the circle.
Active stick — you lift his, then poke the puck free.
You knock it to the corner, kill the rush.
You jump back to the bench. Clean change.

scenarios.title: Net-front tip and bury the rebound
lines: See yourself at the net-front, ready to make a hard play in traffic.
The point man winds up for the shot.
You set up at the net-front, stick on the ice.
You find a lane, body between the D and the goalie.
The shot comes — you get a piece, tip it down.
The goalie kicks out a rebound.
You're already on it, hands quick.
You bang it home before he resets.
The puck is in. Net-front work paid off.

scenarios.title: Clean faceoff win to a shot
lines: See yourself over the dot, set, calm, and ready for the puck drop.
You're at the dot in the O-zone. You set your grip.
You read the linesman's hand. You win it clean back to your D.
You release off the dot and find the soft ice in the slot.
Your D walks the line and the lane opens.
The puck comes down to the tape in the middle.
One touch, you bury it bar-down.

scenarios.title: 2-on-1 read, pass and finish
lines: See the odd-man rush opening up, with space and speed in front of you.
You jump the gap and it's a 2-on-1 the other way.
You read the D — he sinks to take the pass.
You're patient. You drive the soft ice and hold the puck.
He commits to you. The lane to your winger opens.
You slide it flat across, right onto his tape.
He one-times it home. You made that play.

scenarios.title: F1 forecheck, angle and strip
lines: See yourself as F1, first into the zone, setting the pressure.
You're F1, first man in on the forecheck.
You take the right angle and cut off his strong-side option.
You close hard and pin him to the wall.
You lift his stick and steal the puck off the boards.
You spin off pressure, low to the net.
You tuck it short side. Forecheck to goal.

scenarios.title: Offensive-zone cycle and low-to-high play
lines: See the puck below the goal line and feel your line ready to build pressure.
The puck is below the goal line. You get there first.
You protect it with your body, shoulder into pressure.
You hear your teammate call for the cycle.
You leave it soft off the wall and roll high.
The puck moves low to high to your D.
You drive through the defender’s hands to the net-front.
The shot comes through traffic.
You screen the goalie and hunt the rebound.
Possession kept. Pressure built. That is winning hockey.

scenarios.title: 3-on-2 rush with a smart middle-lane drive
lines: See the rush forming, and know your middle-lane drive can open everything.
You cross the red line with speed. It is a 3-on-2.
Your winger has the puck wide. You read the gap.
You drive the middle lane hard, stick on the ice.
Both defenders feel you coming through the slot.
The far winger delays into open ice.
The pass goes across. The shot comes.
You stop at the net-front, ready for the rebound.
The puck kicks loose. You bury it from the top of the crease.

scenarios.title: Win a defensive-zone faceoff responsibility
lines: See the defensive-zone draw and feel the importance of doing your job.
Defensive-zone draw. Late in the period.
You set your feet and know your job.
The puck drops. You tie up hard.
Your winger jumps through and wins the loose puck.
You support low and protect the middle.
The puck goes wall-side, then out.
You clear the zone and get fresh legs on.
Small play. Big moment. You did your job.

scenarios.title: Track and save
lines: See the play enter your zone and feel yourself settled in your crease.
The puck comes up the wall into the zone.
You set your depth, square to the puck.
You stay big, hands out front, weight on the balls of your feet.
The shooter loads up from the top of the circle.
You track it off his blade, all the way in.
You take it in the chest, smother it.
The whistle blows. You've got it.
You're calm. Set for the next one.

scenarios.title: Rebound control to the corner
lines: See the shooter on the wing and feel yourself ready to control the puck.
Shot from the wing, low and hard.
You drop into your butterfly, sealed to the ice.
You take it off the pad.
You angle the pad, steer the rebound to the corner.
No second chance — the puck dies in the boards.
You push back to your feet.
You re-center on your post, depth set.
Eyes back on the puck. Next shot only.

scenarios.title: Save your ground, post to post
lines: See the puck below the goal line and feel yourself sealed and patient.
The puck swings low, below the goal line.
You seal the near post, leak nothing short side.
The pass goes cross-ice, backdoor.
You push post to post, square on arrival.
You're set before the shot — not sliding through it.
You take it on the blocker, controlled.
You direct it high glass, out of danger.
You reset to center. You held your ground.

scenarios.title: Read the breakaway
lines: See the player coming in alone and feel your body stay big and quiet.
He's behind the D, in alone, gathering speed.
You set your depth at the top of the crease.
You match his pace as he comes, staying patient.
Stay big. Hands out front. Don't bite on the first move.
He pulls it to his forehand, tries to go five-hole.
You stay on your feet, then close the seal.
The puck hits your pads and dies.
You cover it up. You won that one-on-one.

scenarios.title: Clean glove save and freeze
lines: See the shot developing through traffic and feel your glove ready out front.
Puck's in the corner, they're working it for a shot.
You're square, gloves out front, tracking it through.
The shot comes hard, glove side, high.
You catch it clean. The leather snaps shut.
You hold it. The whistle goes.
You stayed square. You held it. Faceoff.

scenarios.title: Scramble, desperation post save
lines: See the crease get messy and know you are going to keep fighting for the puck.
It's a scramble in your crease. The puck's loose.
The first shot beats you and you're down and out.
You don't quit on it. You drive to your post.
You throw the pad across and seal the ice.
The puck hits you and stays out.
You found it, you covered it. Whistle. Breathe.

scenarios.title: Track the shot through traffic
lines: See bodies in front of you and feel yourself working to find the puck.
There's a shot coming and a body in your sightline.
You don't fight the screen. You move to find the puck.
You drop and look around the hip, eyes on the release.
You see it late and get a piece — it's off your chest.
You smother the rebound before a stick can find it.
You read it through the traffic. Save and freeze.

scenarios.title: Play the puck and start the breakout
lines: See the dump-in coming and know you can help your team before pressure arrives.
The puck is dumped in hard around the glass.
You leave your crease and get behind it early.
You stop it clean behind the net.
You shoulder-check. F1 is coming.
You make the simple play to your defenseman’s forehand.
He collects it with time. The forecheck slows down.
You get back to your crease, square and ready.
You helped your team break out before the pressure arrived.

scenarios.title: Penalty kill lateral read through seam
lines: See the power play moving the puck and feel yourself patient through the seam.
They are on the power play, moving the puck around the outside.
You stay patient, square to the puck.
The pass goes across the seam.
You push hard, eyes arriving before your body.
You get set on your edge before the release.
The one-timer comes. You take it in the chest.
No rebound. Whistle.
You stayed patient. You beat the pass.

scenarios.title: Pick-and-roll downhill
lines: See yourself bringing the ball up, calm, with the floor spread in front of you.
You bring it up, eyes on the floor.
You call for the screen. Your big climbs up to set it.
You wait for the screen to hit. You do not leave early.
You snake off the pick, shoulder past his man.
The big drops back in coverage.
You see the gap open in the lane.
You get downhill, two feet in the paint.
The low defender steps toward you.
You read it calmly — finish if he stays, pass if he commits.
Tonight, he stays. You lay it in soft off the glass.
Good read. Good pace. Next possession.

scenarios.title: Pull-up in transition
lines: See the rebound hit your hands and feel the floor open in transition.
You snatch the rebound and push.
You see numbers, and the defense is backpedaling.
One dribble, two, eating up the floor.
The defender keeps giving ground.
You feel your feet hit the spot just inside the line.
You rise up, balanced, eyes on the rim.
Clean release.
It drops.
You sprint back, already talking on defense.

scenarios.title: On-ball pressure, live-ball steal
lines: See yourself picking up full court, low, active, and ready to pressure.
You pick up your man full court.
You're in a stance, chest in front, feet active.
He tries to turn the corner.
You cut him off with your chest, not your hands.
He puts his head down to dribble.
You time the bounce, dig down once, and knock it loose.
You scoop it on the run, no one between you and the rim.
You finish it strong on the other end.
Feet first. Hands second. Clean defense.

scenarios.title: Drive and kick
lines: See the closeout coming and know you are ready to attack the advantage.
You catch it at the top, your man closes out hard.
You rip through and attack his front foot.
You turn the corner, downhill into the paint.
The low man slides over to stop the drive.
You see your shooter spot up in the corner.
You hang, draw the second defender.
You whip the kick-out on a rope.
He catches it in rhythm and buries it.
You created the shot by making the right read.

scenarios.title: Press break — calm, simple, advance
lines: See the press coming at you, and feel yourself calm with the ball.
They pick you up full court.
You come back to the ball, low and strong.
Two defenders start to shade you toward the sideline.
You do not panic.
You retreat dribble once and create space.
Your teammate flashes to the middle.
You hit him on time, away from pressure.
The ball moves ahead. The press is broken.
You sprint into spacing and get the team organized.
Calm with the ball. Simple play. Pressure handled.

scenarios.title: Settle the team and run the set
lines: See the game speeding up and feel yourself ready to settle the team.
The game is speeding up. Your team needs a good possession.
You bring it across half court under control.
You raise your hand and call the set.
You move the ball early and cut through.
The ball reverses. The defense shifts.
You get it back and make the simple read.
Your teammate gets a clean look in rhythm.
Good possession. You led without forcing.

scenarios.title: Clutch free throws — split a tie
lines: See yourself at the line in a tight game, with your routine ready.
Tie game, under ten seconds, you drive hard and the whistle blows.
Two shots. The other gym is loud behind the rim.
You step to the line and the ref hands you the ball.
Three dribbles, spin it, breath out — your routine, the same every time.
Legs are tired, but you bend your knees and use them anyway.
Eyes on the back of the rim.
First one is pure.
Same breath, same hold.
Second one drops through.
You backpedal on defense, calm and ready for the next stop.

scenarios.title: Ice the game from the line, off the press
lines: See the trap coming late, and feel yourself strong with the ball.
Up two, final seconds, and they trap you full-court to take the ball.
Two defenders come hard — you stay low, protect the ball, and do not panic.
You keep your dribble alive and get fouled.
They have to put you on the line to stop the clock.
Bonus, two shots. You walk up calm.
Routine, breath, eyes up.
First one is good.
Same rhythm. Second one through.
Game is not over because you celebrated. Game is over because you took care of the ball.

scenarios.title: Catch-and-shoot off the closeout
lines: See yourself spaced on the wing, ready before the ball arrives.
You space to the wing, ready.
The ball swings around the horn.
Your feet are ready before the ball arrives. Hands up, knees loaded.
The pass hits your hands.
The closeout flies at you a beat too late.
You go straight up into your shot.
Smooth release over the contest.
You hold your follow-through.
It drops, and you sprint back on defense.

scenarios.title: Attack the closeout
lines: See the skip pass coming and feel the closeout flying at you.
The skip pass finds you on the wing.
You shot-fake. Your man flies by, out of control.
You rip through and put it on the floor, downhill.
Two dribbles into the gap, into the paint.
The big steps up off your roller.
You read his chest. If he stays back, you finish. If he steps up, you drop it off.
Tonight, he is late.
You gather under the rim.
You finish high off the glass through contact.
Strong attack. Right read.

scenarios.title: Wing denial, deflection, get-out
lines: See your matchup trying to get open, and feel yourself locked into the lane.
Your man wants the ball on the wing.
You're in the passing lane, hand in, denying.
You feel him cut, and you stay attached — ball, you, man.
The pass comes anyway, lazy, into the gap.
You jump the lane and deflect it loose.
You corral it and turn upcourt.
You push it ahead.
Your team gets a clean finish on the break.
Defense created offense.

scenarios.title: Backdoor cut and finish
lines: See your defender overplaying, and feel the backdoor lane open.
You're on the wing, and your man overplays the pass.
You feel him cheat, top hand in the lane.
You plant hard and cut backdoor behind him.
You flash open down the lane, hand ready.
The bounce pass leads you to the rim.
You catch it in stride.
One step, eyes up.
You finish at the rim before the help arrives.
You did not stand. You cut with purpose.

scenarios.title: Closeout, contain, contest
lines: See the ball skip to the corner and feel yourself ready to close out under control.
The ball skips to your man in the corner.
You sprint the first steps, then chop your feet.
High hand. No fly-by.
He shot-fakes. You stay down.
He drives baseline.
You slide and cut him off.
He picks up the ball with nowhere to go.
You contest the pass and your team resets the defense.
Disciplined closeout. No foul. No blow-by.

scenarios.title: Pass, relocate, catch ready
lines: See yourself move after the pass, staying active and ready.
You catch on the wing and the closeout comes hard.
You make the simple pass to the top.
You do not stand and watch.
You slide to the corner.
Your hands are ready. Your feet are already set.
The ball swings back to you.
You catch in rhythm, eyes on the rim.
Smooth release.
It drops, and you sprint back on defense.
Pass. Move. Be ready.

scenarios.title: Clutch free throws — and-1 on the road
lines: See yourself at the line on the road, with the gym trying to shake you.
Late in a road game, you catch on the wing and rise into your jumper.
It falls, and the contact comes — and-1.
The home crowd gets loud, trying to pull you out of your routine.
You step up, settle your feet, and let the noise wash past you.
One dribble, deep breath, the same stroke you shoot in an empty gym.
Elbow in, follow through, hold it.
Bottom of the net.
You turn and sprint back, calm and locked in.

scenarios.title: Pull-up late-game shot over the closeout
lines: See the ball find you late, with space to rise into your shot.
Under thirty seconds, a one-possession game, the ball finds you on the wing.
Your man flies at the closeout, hand up, trying to run you off the line.
One hard jab.
You rise up clean over the contest.
Elbow in, eyes on the rim, the same shot you have practiced over and over.
It leaves your hand soft, perfect rotation.
It drops clean.
You turn and sprint back, already onto the next stop.

scenarios.title: Roll to the rim and finish
lines: See yourself sprinting into the screen, ready to create contact and roll hard.
You climb up and set a wall of a screen.
Your guard rubs his man off you.
You feel the hit, then you roll hard to the rim.
The low man tags you, then recovers out.
You're open, sealing down the middle.
The pocket pass hits you on the roll.
You catch it on the move.
Two feet. Strong base.
You go up strong through the contact.
The ball drops. Strong finish.

scenarios.title: Deep post seal and drop step
lines: See yourself beating your man down the floor and earning deep position.
You sprint the floor and beat your man down.
You did the work before the catch.
You sit him on your back, seal deep, hands high.
Your guard hits you on the block.
You feel where he is playing you — high side.
You drop step baseline into the open lane.
One strong dribble, into your body.
You go up off two feet and finish over him.
Early work. Deep catch. Strong finish.

scenarios.title: Box out, rebound, outlet
lines: See the shot go up and feel yourself ready to own the glass.
The shot goes up from the wing.
You find your man, put a body on him, and hit him first.
You feel him try to slip around.
You stay attached.
The ball comes off long to your side.
You go get it at its highest point, two hands.
You land, chin it, and pivot away from pressure.
You see your guard leaking out.
You hit the outlet ahead and start the break.
The possession ends because you did your work on the glass.

scenarios.title: Rim protection, contest, start the break
lines: See the guard coming downhill and feel yourself ready to protect the rim.
Their guard turns the corner, downhill at you.
You're in drop coverage, walling up the paint.
You hold your ground, feet set, chest square.
He gathers and goes up at the rim.
You meet him vertical, hands high, no reach.
He has to finish over length.
The shot comes off.
You secure it with two hands.
Outlet ahead. Your stop started the break.

scenarios.title: Set the screen, re-screen, create the advantage
lines: See yourself coming into the screen with purpose, ready to create an advantage.
You sprint into the screen, feet set, wide base.
Your guard waits until you make contact.
The defender fights over the top.
You turn and re-screen, changing the angle.
Now your guard gets downhill.
You roll hard into the open lane.
The help steps up.
Your teammate gets the corner three.
Your screen created the shot. That is winning basketball.

scenarios.title: Short roll — catch, read, pass
lines: See the defense step toward your guard and feel the pocket open for you.
You set the screen high. Your guard turns the corner.
Both defenders step toward him.
You slip into the pocket, hands ready.
The pass hits you at the free-throw line.
You land on two feet and read the low man.
He steps up to stop you.
You drop the pass to your teammate under the rim.
Easy finish.
You made the right read.

scenarios.title: Clutch free throws — the hack didn't work
lines: See yourself at the line late, with everyone expecting the miss.
They put you on the line on purpose, late, hoping you'll miss.
The whole gym knows the plan.
You step up slow, set your feet wide, and breathe it all the way down.
Spin the ball, find your line, same routine you grind after every practice.
You shot these when no one was watching.
Push it soft off the fingertips.
First one drops.
Same motion, no rush.
Second one falls clean.
You did your job. Now get back and protect the paint.

scenarios.title: Late rim protection — wall up, no foul
lines: See the final possession coming at the rim, and feel yourself ready to wall up.
Final possession, you're guarding the rim, protecting a two-point lead.
Their guard turns the corner and comes downhill at you full speed.
You slide over, feet set, hands straight up.
Vertical. No reach.
He goes up to finish.
You wall up, stay vertical, and take away the finish.
All contest. No whistle.
The rebound falls to your hands.
You rip it down strong.
Outlet it ahead, and the game is yours.
